Ωedit TypeScript Client Documentation
    Preparing search index...
    • protobuf_ts/generated/omega_edit/v1/omega_edit.grpc-client
    • IEditorServiceClient

    Interface IEditorServiceClient

    EditorService is the primary gRPC service for the Ωedit library. It provides byte-level editing of arbitrarily large files through a session/viewport model with unlimited, serialised undo/redo.

    Core concepts

    • Session – An editing context backed by an optional file. All edits (insert, delete, overwrite) are applied to a session. Sessions support checkpointing, transactions, and save-to-disk.

    • Viewport – A sliding window into a session's computed content. Viewports can be floating (automatically follow edits) or fixed (pinned to a byte offset). Multiple viewports may observe the same session.

    • Change – A single atomic edit (insert, delete, or overwrite). Every change receives a monotonically increasing serial number and can be undone or redone.

    • Event stream – Server-push notifications for session and viewport state changes, delivered via gRPC server-streaming RPCs.

    Generated

    from protobuf service omega_edit.v1.EditorService

    createSession

    • createSession(
          input: CreateSessionRequest,
          metadata: Metadata,
          options: CallOptions,
          callback: (err: ServiceError | null, value?: CreateSessionResponse) => void,
      ): SurfaceCall

      Create a new editing session, optionally backed by an existing file. Returns the assigned session ID, checkpoint directory, and (if a file was provided) the original file size. If the server has begun shutdown or is draining existing sessions, this RPC fails with UNAVAILABLE.

      Parameters

      • input: CreateSessionRequest
      • metadata: Metadata
      • options: CallOptions
      • callback: (err: ServiceError | null, value?: CreateSessionResponse) => void

      Returns SurfaceCall

    getByteFrequencyProfile

    • getByteFrequencyProfile(
          input: GetByteFrequencyProfileRequest,
          metadata: Metadata,
          options: CallOptions,
          callback: (
              err: ServiceError | null,
              value?: GetByteFrequencyProfileResponse,
          ) => void,
      ): SurfaceCall

      Compute a byte-frequency histogram (256 buckets, one per byte value) over a range of session data. Bucket 256 counts DOS-style CR+LF line endings.

      Parameters

      • input: GetByteFrequencyProfileRequest
      • metadata: Metadata
      • options: CallOptions
      • callback: (err: ServiceError | null, value?: GetByteFrequencyProfileResponse) => void

    saveSession

    • saveSession(
          input: SaveSessionRequest,
          metadata: Metadata,
          options: CallOptions,
          callback: (err: ServiceError | null, value?: SaveSessionResponse) => void,
      ): SurfaceCall

      Persist the session's computed content to disk. The caller specifies the target path and IO flags (overwrite behaviour). An optional byte range can limit the save to a sub-section of the data.

      Parameters

      • input: SaveSessionRequest
      • metadata: Metadata
      • options: CallOptions
      • callback: (err: ServiceError | null, value?: SaveSessionResponse) => void

              value?: SessionBeginTransactionResponse,

      • metadata: Metadata
      • options: CallOptions
      • callback: (err: ServiceError | null, value?: SessionBeginTransactionResponse) => void

          input: SubscribeToSessionEventsRequest,

      ): ClientReadableStream<SubscribeToSessionEventsResponse>

      Subscribe to a server-streaming feed of session-level events (edits, undos, saves, viewport creation/destruction, etc.). The interest bitmask controls which event kinds are delivered.

      Parameters

      • input: SubscribeToSessionEventsRequest
      • Optionalmetadata: Metadata
      • Optionaloptions: CallOptions
